name: Aspartame
health_note: Generally recognized as safe, but debated. Must be avoided by individuals with phenylketonuria (PKU).

name: Monosodium Glutamate (MSG)
health_note: Considered safe by FDA, though some people report sensitivity symptoms.

name: Sodium Benzoate
health_note: Inhibits growth of mold and bacteria. Some studies suggest a link to hyperactivity when combined with food dyes.

name: High Fructose Corn Syrup
health_note: Linked to obesity and insulin resistance when consumed in high amounts.

name: Carrageenan
health_note: Derived from seaweed. Some concerns about digestive inflammation in sensitive individuals.

name: Yellow 5 (Tartrazine)
health_note: May cause allergic reactions in some people and linked to hyperactivity in children.

name: Titanium Dioxide
health_note: Recently banned in the EU as a food additive due to concerns about DNA damage.

name: Xanthan Gum
health_note: Generally safe. Can act as a prebiotic or a laxative in very high doses.
